annotations.note: Mathlib defines the n-th Fourier coefficient of a function on `AddCircle T` via `fourierCoeff`, together with the exponential monomials `fourier n`.

annotations.label: Partial sums of a Fourier series

anchor.snippet: A Fourier series can be written in several equivalent forms

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses convergence through `HasSum` of `fourierCoeff f i • fourier i` rather than naming partial sums explicitly.

anchor.snippet: Consider a sawtooth function

annotations.note: No formalization of the sawtooth-function example was found in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Mathlib uses only the complex exponential form; an amplitude-phase reformulation is not present.

annotations.label: Basel problem via Parseval

anchor.snippet: Another application is to solve the Basel problem

annotations.note: `hasSum_zeta_two` proves ∑ 1/n² = π²/6 in Mathlib using Fourier-series machinery (`hasSum_one_div_nat_pow_mul_fourier`).

annotations.label: Symmetry of Fourier transform components

anchor.snippet: The transform of a real-valued function

annotations.note: Mathlib has `fourier_neg` (conjugation symmetry of monomials), but no packaged lemma on real/even/odd-part symmetries of Fourier coefficients.

annotations.label: Riemann–Lebesgue lemma

anchor.snippet: is integrable

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Riemann–Lebesgue lemma on finite-dimensional real vector spaces, which specializes to vanishing Fourier coefficients at infinity.

annotations.label: Parseval's theorem for Fourier series

anchor.snippet: periodic over an interval of length

annotations.note: `hasSum_sq_fourierCoeff` (and `hasSum_sq_fourierCoeffOn`) gives Parseval's identity for L² functions on `AddCircle T`.

anchors.snippet: periodic over an interval of length

annotations.label: Plancherel's theorem (Riesz–Fischer)

anchor.snippet: then there is a unique function

annotations.note: `fourierBasis` is a `HilbertBasis ℤ ℂ (Lp ℂ 2 haarAddCircle)`, giving the isometric isomorphism with ℓ²(ℤ,ℂ).

annotations.label: Fourier coefficients of pointwise product

anchor.snippet: discrete convolution of the

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib expresses `fourierCoeff (f*g)` as the discrete convolution of `fourierCoeff f` and `fourierCoeff g` on `AddCircle T`.

annotations.label: Fourier coefficients of periodic convolution

anchor.snippet: periodic convolution

annotations.note: Mathlib has Fourier-transform convolution theorems (`Real.fourier_bilin_convolution_eq`) but no analogue for Fourier coefficients on `AddCircle T`.

annotations.label: Characterization of Fourier coefficient sequences

anchor.snippet: doubly infinite sequence

annotations.note: Surjectivity of `fourierBasis.repr` onto ℓ²(ℤ,ℂ) gives the L² characterization implicitly but is not stated as a standalone theorem.

annotations.label: Fourier coefficients of the derivative

anchor.snippet: times differentiable

annotations.note: Mathlib has `fourier_deriv` for the Fourier transform, but no derivative formula for `fourierCoeff` on `AddCircle T`.

annotations.label: Fourier series on a compact group

anchor.snippet: one can produce Fourier series on any compact group

annotations.note: Mathlib develops Fourier series only on `AddCircle T` and finite abelian groups, not on general compact groups.

annotations.label: Peter–Weyl theorem

anchor.snippet: Peter–Weyl theorem

annotations.note: No occurrence of Peter–Weyl was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fourier series on a Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: the natural generalization is to use the eigensolutions of the Laplace–Beltrami operator

annotations.note: No Laplace–Beltrami / spherical-harmonics Fourier-expansion framework is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fourier transform on locally compact Abelian groups

anchor.snippet: straightforward generalization to Locally Compact Abelian

annotations.note: Mathlib handles the finite-abelian case via `PontryaginDuality`, but no general LCA-group Fourier transform is defined.

anchor.snippet: the series is sometimes described as a Fourier-Schwartz series

annotations.note: Mathlib has tempered-distribution Fourier transforms but not a `AddCircle`-distribution Fourier-series framework.

annotations.label: Fourier series in Hilbert space L^2

anchor.snippet: the space of square-integrable functions

annotations.note: `fourierBasis` realizes the L² Fourier-series decomposition as a Hilbert basis indexed by ℤ.

annotations.label: Sines and cosines form an orthonormal basis of L^2

anchor.snippet: the sines and cosines form an orthogonal set

annotations.note: Mathlib proves orthonormality of complex exponentials (`orthonormal_fourier`) and that they form a Hilbert basis, but not the real sin/cos version explicitly.

anchors.snippet: the sines and cosines form an orthogonal set

annotations.label: Best trigonometric polynomial approximation
annotations.note: The best-approximation property follows from `fourierBasis` being a Hilbert basis (orthogonal projection onto its span), but is not stated specifically for trig polynomials.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence for C^1 functions via Cauchy–Schwarz

anchor.snippet: absolutely summable

annotations.note: Mathlib proves uniform convergence under summability of Fourier coefficients but does not derive this directly from a C¹ hypothesis.

annotations.label: Carleson's theorem

anchor.snippet: Carleson's theorem

annotations.note: Carleson's theorem on a.e. convergence of L² Fourier series is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: L^2 convergence of Fourier series
annotations.note: `hasSum_fourier_series_L2` proves convergence of the Fourier series to f in the L²-norm on `AddCircle T`.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence of Fourier series
annotations.note: `hasSum_fourier_series_of_summable` gives uniform convergence of the Fourier series whenever the coefficient sequence is summable.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov's everywhere-divergent Fourier series

anchor.snippet: Andrey Kolmogorov published an article

annotations.note: Kolmogorov's construction of an L¹ function with everywhere-divergent Fourier series is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous function with Fourier series diverging at 0

anchor.snippet: It is possible to give explicit examples of a continuous function whose Fourier series diverges at 0

annotations.note: No explicit example of a continuous function with Fourier series diverging at 0 was found in Mathlib.
